query_normalized: memslides: a hierarchical memory driven agent framework for personalized slide generation with multi-turn local revision linguistics
rationale: The paper investigates how discourse relations are distributed within and across turns in spontaneous conversation, providing empirical insight into multi‑turn linguistic structures (category a: same‑mechanism evidence). This aligns with the “multi‑turn local revision linguistics” aspect of the MemSlides framework, even though it does not address slide generation or memory‑driven agents directly.
